# Glossary

These are initial working definitions derived from the handoff packet. They are not final source-derived definitions.

| Term | Working definition | Status |
|---|---|---|
| ASI stack | A proposed substrate-neutral governed architecture of logical responsibility and authority boundaries across alignment, governance, planning, memory, reasoning, execution, routing, compression, evidence, and improvement. A monolithic model, modular service graph, hybrid system, or human/AI process may instantiate the boundaries; the term does not require one model or physical module per layer. | working architecture definition |
| Alignment and constitution | The layer that constrains admissible goals, values, self-modification, and human-agency requirements. | initial; refine after source ingestion |
| Stable Capability Field | A stable capability boundary with replaceable implementations, bounded authority, qualification evidence, and rollback rules. | initial; refine after source ingestion |
| PlanForge | The planning/control framing for compiling goals into governed task graphs, dependencies, budgets, and replanning triggers. | initial; refine after source ingestion |
| Virtual Context Memory | A governed memory/context layer that compiles evidence-carrying context packets from durable sources. | initial; refine after source ingestion |
| Talos | The labor/execution OS framing for typed jobs, artifact graphs, tool permissions, audit logs, and replay. | initial; refine after source ingestion |
| Spinoza | The reasoning and verification framing for claim extraction, evidence tiers, contradiction handling, and belief revision. | initial; refine after source ingestion |
| MoECOT | A modular orchestration/prototype framing with specialist cores, readiness gates, ledgers, and replay. | initial; refine after source ingestion |
| Octopus Router | A lightweight routing architecture for dynamically selecting bounded specialist arms or cores. | initial; refine after source ingestion |
| Ratcheting Modular Intelligence | A capability-growth doctrine based on benchmark frontiers, residual escrow, regression preservation, and verified module promotion. | initial; refine after source ingestion |
| Compact Generative Systems | A compression framing focused on the smallest adequate generative or governing structure without hiding residual complexity. | initial; refine after source ingestion |
| RankFold / NeuralFold | A compression source family for low-rank residual coding, functional preprocessing, and fallback routing. | initial; refine after source ingestion |
| BBVCA | A generate-verify-repair compression source family using seeded local laws, bounded search, and residual tracking. | initial; refine after source ingestion |
| Artifact graph | A traceable graph of produced objects, source handles, jobs, claims, and evidence records. | initial; refine after source ingestion |
| Claim ledger | A structured record of claims, support states, sources, uncertainty, contradiction links, and review status. | initial; refine after source ingestion |
| Context packet | The active, compiled context supplied to an agent or job, including source handles, summaries, authority labels, and adequacy status. | initial; refine after source ingestion |
| Proof-carrying summary | A summary that carries provenance and checks sufficient to audit its fidelity to source material. | initial; refine after source ingestion |
| Authority ceiling | The maximum action authority a layer, field, tool, or implementation may exercise. | initial; refine after source ingestion |
| Readiness gate | An evidence and regression checkpoint that must pass before a module or field can be promoted. | initial; refine after source ingestion |
| Residual escrow | A visible backlog of unresolved failures, uncertainty, or residual complexity left by a route, compression, or benchmark result. | initial; refine after source ingestion |
| Benchmark ratchet | A process that uses tests, regressions, hidden checks, and residuals to move capability claims without erasing failure cases. | initial; refine after source ingestion |
